what_it_does: Defines a Pydantic data contract for one discovered capability of an external platform: an explicit EntryKind literal, a CapabilityVerbs edge layer, and the CapabilityEntry model that holds identity, flag/tool/setting/hook metadata, posture classification, supervisor assembly hints, and provenance fields.
observations: EntryKind is a Literal of 14 capability kinds with a 'novel kind FAILS LOUD' policy.
CapabilityVerbs exposes readable/searchable/projectable/configurable booleans, mostly defaulting True except configurable.
CapabilityEntry.id is constructed as f'{kind}/{name}', and for flags the name includes the '--' prefix.
The model mixes many capability shapes into one flat schema: flag fields, command/tool schemas, setting fields, hook fields, etc.
Posture is a Literal of locked/hazard/consent/safe/unmatched with a posture_rule field to record which rule fired.
Supervisor-specific fields assembler_kind and locked_reason are present for SpawnFlagAssembler integration.
version is set to 1 and explicitly called out as the registry-type convention, not schema_ver.
platform_id defaults to 'claude-code' as a foreign-key-style field to PlatformEntry.
Provenance fields include source, source_url, fetched_at, discovered_at, status, retired_in_version, and raw_extra.
raw_extra captures init-event fields outside the known set rather than dropping them.
standouts: The typed Literal kind registry that refuses to silently accept unknown kinds.
The posture/posture_rule/axis classification fields that make the derivation auditable.
The id construction rule that embeds the kind and preserves '--' for flags.
CapabilityVerbs as a separate 'verb-edge' mini-model attached to every entry.
The heavy inline documentation tying fields to build-plan fixes and spec sections.
conventions: Uses Pydantic BaseModel with type annotations and default values.
Follows an additive schema-versioning convention (version int, optional fields).
Uses default mutable containers (list/dict) which Pydantic handles per-field.
Docstrings and comments carry design rationale and external spec references.
concerns: A single flat model spans many unrelated capability shapes, which may encourage sparse rows and weak invariants.
Posture is described as DERIVED yet stored directly on the entry, creating a risk of stale or uncomputed values.
There is no runtime validation enforcing the id format, kind/name consistency, or flag prefix rules.
value_type and assembler_kind are free strings rather than Literals, weakening the fail-loud vocabulary goal.
raw_extra is a dict default that could accumulate unvalidated data and obscure schema drift.
notes: A registry-type Pydantic contract that tries to make every discovered platform capability a single auditable, projectable row with explicit posture and supervisor assembly metadata.
questions: Which component actually computes posture and writes posture_rule?
How is the 'novel kind FAILS LOUD' policy enforced at ingestion time?
What guarantees id uniqueness across kinds and platforms?
How is raw_extra reconciled when fields later become first-class?
Is PlatformEntry defined in a separate file and how is the FK relationship validated?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to be the single source all faces project from for discovered capabilities; the file delivers that as a schema/contract rather than active logic, which matches the intent.
intention_signals: The file is trying to create a stable, machine-discoverable ledger row for each external capability so that multiple faces (MCP, bridge, CLI) can read, search, and assemble the same capability consistently and safely.
novelty: The combination of a fail-loud typed kind literal, auditable posture rule tracking, supervisor flag-assembly metadata (assembler_kind/locked_reason), and an id rule that preserves CLI flag prefixes inside the identifier.
contribution: Provides a canonical Pydantic shape for capability registry entries, including a closed kind vocabulary, verb-edge layer, posture classification fields, supervisor assembly hints, and provenance metadata, which downstream registry, search, and assembly code can rely on.
summary_for_embedding: Pydantic data contract defining a registry row (CapabilityEntry) for discovered platform capabilities, with a typed kind literal, verb-edge layer, posture classification, supervisor assembly metadata, and provenance fields.
intention_for_embedding: To serve as the single source-of-truth schema that all faces project from when representing discovered capabilities of an external platform.

symbols.does: Defines the verb-edge layer that states which operations are valid over a capability entry.
symbols.description: CapabilityVerbs is a small Pydantic model holding readable, searchable, projectable, and configurable booleans. It defaults to exposing capabilities (True) except configurable, mirroring a fractal type-fields-verb-edge shape and letting consumers know whether an entry can be fetched, searched, projected, or modified.

symbols.does: Defines one discovered capability row in the mirror registry.
symbols.description: CapabilityEntry is the central Pydantic model for a discovered platform capability. It carries a composite id (kind/name), a closed EntryKind, name/aliases/description, kind-specific fields for flags, commands, tools, settings, and hooks, plus derived posture classification, supervisor assembly hints (assembler_kind, locked_reason), provenance metadata, a registry-style version, and an attached CapabilityVerbs object.
